operating_state: Total revenue reached $117.9 billion in 2021, representing 37% year-over-year growth driven by Family of Apps advertising revenue increasing 36% as ad impressions rose 10% and average price per ad climbed 24%. Daily active people across the family of apps grew 8% to 2.82 billion with user growth concentrated in emerging markets, though growth rates are decelerating due to platform maturity and TikTok competition. Reality Labs revenue doubled but remains nascent relative to core advertising business.
cost_structure: Total expenses increased 34% with research and development rising 42% reflecting significant metaverse investments and 30% engineering headcount growth. Cost of revenue increased 36% driven by Reality Labs hardware costs and data center infrastructure expansion, partially offset by extended server asset useful lives reducing depreciation. Reality Labs posted operating losses expanding to $10.2 billion from $6.6 billion prior year, reducing overall operating profit by approximately $10 billion.
material_events: Meta completed corporate rebranding from Facebook Inc. to Meta Platforms Inc. in October 2021, signaling strategic pivot toward metaverse development with new two-segment reporting structure separating Family of Apps and Reality Labs. The company executed $44.8 billion in share repurchases during 2021, retiring 136 million Class A shares, with board authorizing additional $75 billion in repurchase capacity. A significant six-hour service outage occurred in October 2021 while the company faced intensified media scrutiny following internal document releases regarding algorithms and content enforcement practices.
macro_exposures: Apple's iOS privacy changes implemented in 2021 materially reduced advertising targeting and measurement capabilities, creating revenue headwinds in the second half with continued adverse impact expected. European data transfer frameworks face disruption following Privacy Shield invalidation, with Irish Data Protection Commission inquiry potentially prohibiting EU-US data transfers as early as first half 2022. Foreign exchange provided $1.6 billion favorable revenue impact while COVID-19 pandemic effects continue creating workforce productivity challenges and supply chain disruptions affecting data center equipment procurement.
forward_outlook: Management committed to investing $10+ billion annually in Reality Labs for metaverse development over the next several years, with 2022 total expenses projected at $90-95 billion and year-over-year expense growth significantly exceeding revenue growth rates. Capital expenditures guidance reflects continued data center and technical infrastructure expansion with continued hiring across engineering and administrative functions while implementing end-to-end encryption across messaging services.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple antitrust proceedings remain pending including FTC litigation seeking forced divestiture of Instagram and WhatsApp acquisitions, with court denying motion to dismiss FTC amended complaint in January 2022. Irish Data Protection Commission final decision on European data transfers could materially impact European operations, potentially requiring suspension of services in Europe. IRS transfer pricing disputes could result in up to $9.7 billion in additional federal tax liability plus interest and penalties, while metaverse strategy success remains uncertain given limited consumer hardware experience.
